pred_reason: SWC-105 is appropriate because the dispersePaymentForShares function transfers ETH to addresses derived from unvalidated user input, allowing arbitrary address control. This directly matches the SWC-105 vulnerability pattern of uncontrolled ETH withdrawal to arbitrary destinations. Evidence: dispersePaymentForShares sends ETH to addresses from minerPayoutData array without validation; User-controlled input can specify arbitrary recipient addresses for ETH transfers; Impact allows draining contract funds through malicious minerPayoutData entries
